title: Grafana 12.0 — Grafana-managed Recording Rules
description: Grafana-managed recording rules went GA in 12.0. This dashboard shows the canonical pattern: an expensive raw metric alongside a cheaper recorded version. Create a recording rule at /alerting/new/recording and point it at the raw expression on the left; then wire the resulting series into the right-hand panel.

options.content:
DEMO GUIDE
Grafana 12.0 — Grafana-managed Recording Rules
🎯 What you're looking at
Grafana now evaluates recording rules itself — no need for a separate Mimir/Prometheus ruler in OSS.
🧪 Try this
Click the link in the intro to create a recording rule, then come back and refresh. The right-hand panel will start showing the recorded metric.
📚 Learn more: Grafana docs · v12 What's New

### Grafana-managed recording rules

In 12.0 Grafana can now evaluate your recording rules itself (no Mimir/Prometheus ruler needed for OSS).

1. Enable toggle: `grafanaManagedRecordingRules` ✓ (on)
2. Create the rule → [Alerting › New recording rule](/alerting/new/recording)
3. Suggested recipe:
   - **Name:** `grafana:http_p95_5m`
   - **Expression:** `histogram_quantile(0.95, sum by (le, handler)(rate(grafana_http_request_duration_seconds_bucket[5m])))`
   - **Evaluation group:** `grafana-slo`, every `30s`
4. The right-hand panel below queries the recorded metric by name — once the rule starts firing values, the two panels should match (with lower query cost).

panels.title: Raw query (what you'd normally pay for)
panels.description: Raw query (what you'd normally pay for). Reference: https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/alerting/alerting-rules/create-grafana-managed-rule/

panels.title: Recorded metric (after you create the rule)

defaults.noValue: Create the recording rule to populate this panel
